Description:
This project modifies the get blue barrel block by having the robot celebrate once it has the blue barrel</comment><block type="aim_logic_when_started" id=".NsXwU_L.OuU1l7Sho$F" x="70" y="110"><next><block type="aim_motion_set_turn_velocity" id=";`%;:GI}de[%WNd_L]9;"><value name="VELOCITY"><shadow type="math_number" id="s5nh5e_SG6UzR3YN?dr*"><field name="NUM">40</field></shadow></value><next><block type="aim_macro_turn_until" id="M4KuZ)x9s9u{/}Q}(QCI"><field name="DIRECTION">left</field><field name="OBJECTNAME">BLUE_BARREL</field><next><block type="mixed_multiline_command" id="3@;~lAyjhDBV^6X@q]@#"><field name="MULTILINE_INPUT"># This is an updated get blue barrel block&#10;# Get blue barrel and celebrate&#10;while True:&#10;    vision_data = robot.vision.get_data(BLUE_BARREL)&#10;&#10;    if vision_data[0].exists:&#10;        if robot.has_blue_barrel():&#10;            robot.stop_all_movement()&#10;            # Add celebration&#10;            robot.screen.show_emoji(EXCITED, LOOK_FORWARD)&#10;            robot.sound.play(TADA)&#10;            break&#10;        else:&#10;            robot.move_at(vision_data[0].bearing)&#10;    else:&#10;        robot.move_at(0)&#10;    wait(20, MSEC)</field></block></next></block></next></block></next></block></xml>

#region VEXcode Generated Robot Configuration
from vex import *
from vex_globals import *
import math
import random

# Robot initialization for AIM platform
robot = Robot()

# Controller initialization
controller = Onestick()

# AI Vision configuration code
robot.vision.model_detection(True)
robot.vision.tag_detection(True)

# Console Colors
def set_console_text_color(COLOR):
    if (COLOR == Color.RED):
        print("\033[31m", end="")
    elif (COLOR == Color.GREEN):
        print("\033[32m", end="")
    elif (COLOR == Color.BLUE):
        print("\033[34m", end="")
    elif (COLOR == Color.BLACK):
        print("\033[30m", end="")
    elif (COLOR == Color.WHITE):
        print("\033[37m", end="")
    elif (COLOR == Color.YELLOW):
        print("\033[33m", end="")
    elif (COLOR == Color.ORANGE):
        print("\033[91m", end="")
    elif (COLOR == Color.PURPLE):
        print("\033[35m", end="")
    elif (COLOR == Color.CYAN):
        print("\033[36m", end="")
    elif (COLOR == Color.TRANSPARENT):
        print("\033[97m", end="")
    else:
        print("\033[30m", end="")

# Clear Console
def clear_console():
    print("\033[2J", end="")

# User Uploaded Images
IMAGE1 = "image1.png"
IMAGE2 = "image2.png"
IMAGE3 = "image3.png"
IMAGE4 = "image4.png"
IMAGE5 = "image5.png"
IMAGE6 = "image6.png"
IMAGE7 = "image7.png"
IMAGE8 = "image8.png"
IMAGE9 = "image9.png"
IMAGE10 = "image10.png"


# User Uploaded Sounds
SOUND1 = "sound1.mp3"
SOUND2 = "sound2.mp3"
SOUND3 = "sound3.mp3"
SOUND4 = "sound4.mp3"
SOUND5 = "sound5.mp3"
SOUND6 = "sound6.mp3"
SOUND7 = "sound7.mp3"
SOUND8 = "sound8.mp3"
SOUND9 = "sound9.mp3"
SOUND10 = "sound10.mp3"


# 
set_console_text_color(BLUE)
# Wait until the Inertial Sensor is calibrated
if robot.inertial.is_calibrating():
    robot.screen.clear_screen(1, 1, ORANGE)
    robot.screen.set_pen_color(BLACK)
    robot.screen.set_fill_color(ORANGE)
    robot.screen.set_font(PROP40)
    robot.screen.print_at("Place AIM on", x=41, y=80)
    robot.screen.print_at("a flat surface", x=38, y=120)
    robot.screen.print_at("to calibrate", x=50, y=160)
    while robot.inertial.is_calibrating():
        wait(5, MSEC)
else:
    wait(300, MSEC)

# Restore text / background defaults
robot.screen.set_pen_color(WHITE)
robot.screen.set_fill_color(BLUE)
robot.screen.set_font(MONO24)
robot.screen.clear_screen(1, 1, BLUE)

# Reset the Inertial Sensor heading and rotation
robot.inertial.reset_heading()
robot.inertial.reset_rotation()

#endregion VEXcode Generated Robot Configuration

"""
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Project:      Object Detection Alert
Description:  This project will move the AIM robot forward until any cargo is found.
              When detected, the robot will play a beep sound, and stop all robot movement.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
"""

# Begin project code
while True:
    robot.move_at(0)
    vision_data = robot.vision.get_data(ALL_CARGO)
    if vision_data[0].exists:
        robot.sound.play(CHIRP)
        while robot.sound.is_active():
            wait(50, MSEC)
        robot.stop_all_movement()
        break
    wait(5, MSEC)

              and then sets the AIM LEDs to that selected color

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
"""

# Begin project code

if "red" in answer:
    robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, RED)
elif "green" in answer:
    robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, GREEN)
elif "blue" in answer:
    robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, BLUE)
else:
    print("Did not recognize inputted color.")

"""
--------------------------------------------------------------------

Project:        Creating Timeouts
Description:    This project will turns in place while searching for
                an orange barrel. If none is found within 10 seconds,
                it plays a 'fail' sound and stops the search.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
"""

# Begin project code
stop_searching = False
object_found = False

# Function to end the search
def stop_searching_after_timeout():
    global stop_searching
    stop_searching = True

# Register a timer event that calls our timeout function after 10 seconds (10,000 milliseconds)
timer.event(stop_searching_after_timeout, 10000)

# Pause briefly to ensure the timer event is registered
wait(15, MSEC) 

robot.screen.show_aivision()
print("Searching for an orange barrel...")

robot.set_turn_velocity(30, PERCENT)
robot.turn(RIGHT)

while True:
    vision_data = robot.vision.get_data(ORANGE_BARREL)
    
    if vision_data[0].exists:
        print("Found an orange barrel!")
        object_found = True
        robot.stop_all_movement()
        break

    if stop_searching:
        print("Could not find an orange barrel!")
        robot.stop_all_movement()
        robot.sound.play(FAIL)
        while robot.sound.is_active():
            wait(50, MSEC)
        break

    wait(5, MSEC)

# Callback function when controller up button is pressed
def controller_button_up_pressed():
    robot.screen.show_emoji(CONFIDENT, LOOK_FORWARD)

    # Wait until up button is released to show happy emoji
    while controller.button_up.pressing():
        wait(5, MSEC)
    robot.screen.show_emoji(HAPPY, LOOK_FORWARD)

# Callback function when controller down button is pressed
def controller_button_down_pressed():
    robot.screen.show_emoji(PROUD, LOOK_FORWARD)
    robot.kicker.place()

    # Wait until down button is released to show happy emoji
    while controller.button_down.pressing():
        wait(5, MSEC)
    robot.screen.show_emoji(HAPPY, LOOK_FORWARD)

# Callback function when controller left button is pressed
def controller_button_left_pressed():
    robot.screen.show_emoji(SURPRISED, LOOK_FORWARD)
    robot.sound.play(TURN_LEFT)
    while robot.sound.is_active():
        wait(50, MSEC)

    # Wait until left button is released to show happy emoji
    while controller.button_left.pressing():
        wait(5, MSEC)
    robot.screen.show_emoji(HAPPY, LOOK_FORWARD)

# Callback function when controller right button is pressed
def controller_button_right_pressed():
    robot.screen.show_emoji(SURPRISED, LOOK_FORWARD)
    robot.sound.play(TURN_RIGHT)
    while robot.sound.is_active():
        wait(50, MSEC)

    # Wait until right button is released to show happy emoji
    while controller.button_right.pressing():
        wait(5, MSEC)
    robot.screen.show_emoji(HAPPY, LOOK_FORWARD)

# Callback function when controller stick button is pressed
def controller_button_stick_pressed():
    robot.screen.show_emoji(THRILLED, LOOK_FORWARD)
    robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, BLUE)

    # Wait until stick button is released to show happy emoji
    while controller.button_stick.pressing():
        wait(5, MSEC)
    robot.screen.show_emoji(HAPPY, LOOK_FORWARD)
    robot.led.off(ALL_LEDS)

# Register event handlers and pass callback functions
controller.button_up.pressed(controller_button_up_pressed)
controller.button_stick.pressed(controller_button_stick_pressed)
controller.button_left.pressed(controller_button_left_pressed)
controller.button_down.pressed(controller_button_down_pressed)
controller.button_right.pressed(controller_button_right_pressed)

# add 15ms delay to make sure events are registered correctly.
wait(15, MSEC)

# Always show happy emoji
robot.screen.show_emoji(HAPPY, LOOK_FORWARD)

while True:
    # Move with controller constantly
    robot.move_with_vectors(
        controller.axis1.position() * 1.5,
        0,
        controller.axis2.position() * 0.7,
    )

    # If / Else If / Else statement used to check button presses
    if controller.button_up.pressing():
        # Conditional based on pressing up button
        robot.kicker.place()
        robot.screen.show_emoji(EXCITED, LOOK_FORWARD)
    elif controller.button_down.pressing():
        # Conditional based on pressing down button
        robot.kicker.kick(HARD)
        robot.screen.show_emoji(PROUD, LOOK_FORWARD)
    elif controller.button_left.pressing():
        # Conditional based on pressing left button
        while not not controller.button_left.pressing():
            wait(5, MSEC)
        robot.screen.show_emoji(THINKING, LOOK_FORWARD)

        # Turn left until sports ball is detected
        robot.turn(LEFT)
        while True:
            vision_data = robot.vision.get_data(SPORTS_BALL)

            if vision_data[0].exists:
                # Turn to the object by adding your current heading and the vision bearing offset
                robot.turn_to(robot.inertial.get_heading() + vision_data[0].bearing)
                break
            wait(20, MSEC)

        # Get sports ball
        while True:
            vision_data = robot.vision.get_data(SPORTS_BALL)

            if vision_data[0].exists:
                if robot.has_sports_ball():
                    robot.stop_all_movement()
                    break
                else:
                    robot.move_at(vision_data[0].bearing)
            else:
                robot.move_at(0)
            wait(20, MSEC)

    elif controller.button_right.pressing():
        # Conditional based on pressing right button
        while not not controller.button_right.pressing():
            wait(5, MSEC)
        robot.screen.show_emoji(THINKING, LOOK_FORWARD)

        # Turn right until sports ball is detected
        robot.turn(RIGHT)
        while True:
            vision_data = robot.vision.get_data(SPORTS_BALL)

            if vision_data[0].exists:
                # Turn to the object by adding your current heading and the vision bearing offset
                robot.turn_to(robot.inertial.get_heading() + vision_data[0].bearing)
                break
            wait(20, MSEC)

        # Get sports ball
        while True:
            vision_data = robot.vision.get_data(SPORTS_BALL)

            if vision_data[0].exists:
                if robot.has_sports_ball():
                    robot.stop_all_movement()
                    break
                else:
                    robot.move_at(vision_data[0].bearing)
            else:
                robot.move_at(0)
            wait(20, MSEC)

    else:
        robot.screen.show_emoji(HAPPY, LOOK_FORWARD)
    wait(5, MSEC)

"""

Project:      Move to AprilTag
Description:  This project turns the robot until it detects
              AprilTag 1, then moves toward it

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
"""

# Begin project code

# Turn right until AprilTag ID 1 is detected
robot.turn(RIGHT)
while True:
    vision_data = robot.vision.get_data(TAG1)

    if vision_data[0].exists:
        # Turn to the object by adding your current heading and the vision bearing offset
        robot.turn_to(robot.inertial.get_heading() + vision_data[0].bearing)
        break
    wait(20, MSEC)

# Move to AprilTag ID 1
while True:
    vision_data = robot.vision.get_data(TAG1)

    if vision_data[0].exists:
        if vision_data[0].width >= 60:
            robot.stop_all_movement()
            break
        else:

              moves backwards, and places it

while True:
    vision_data = robot.vision.get_data(ORANGE_BARREL)

    if vision_data[0].exists:
        # Turn to the object by adding your current heading and the vision bearing offset
        robot.turn_to(robot.inertial.get_heading() + vision_data[0].bearing)
        break
    wait(20, MSEC)

# Get orange barrel
while True:
    vision_data = robot.vision.get_data(ORANGE_BARREL)

    if vision_data[0].exists:
        if robot.has_orange_barrel():
            robot.stop_all_movement()
            break
        else:
            robot.move_at(vision_data[0].bearing)
    else:
        robot.move_at(0)
    wait(20, MSEC)

# Reverse 200 mm and place the orange barrel
robot.move_for(200, 180)
robot.kicker.place()

   # act happy

    robot.screen.show_emoji(HAPPY, LOOK_RIGHT)
    robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, YELLOW)
    robot.turn_for(RIGHT, 4, 100)
    robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, WHITE)
    robot.screen.show_emoji(HAPPY, LOOK_LEFT)
    robot.turn_for(RIGHT, -8, 100)
    robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, YELLOW)
    robot.screen.show_emoji(HAPPY, LOOK_RIGHT)
    robot.turn_for(RIGHT, 4, 100)

    wait(100,MSEC)
    for i in range(3):
        robot.screen.show_emoji(QUIET)
        robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, WHITE)
        robot.move_for(1,-90)
        robot.screen.show_emoji(HAPPY)
        robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, YELLOW)
        robot.move_for(1,90)

    robot.stop_all_movement()
    robot.led.off(ALL_LEDS)
    wait(.5,SECONDS) 

def act_sad():

    robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, Color(0, 0, 250))

    robot.screen.show_emoji(SAD, LOOK_FORWARD)
    robot.move_for(11, 45, 70)
    wait(.5,SECONDS)
    robot.led.off(ALL_LEDS)

    robot.turn_for(RIGHT, 360, 100, wait=False)
    face_list = [SILLY, HAPPY, EXCITED, PROUD, THRILLED, LAUGHING]
    colors = [BLUE, CYAN, GREEN, ORANGE, PURPLE, RED, WHITE, YELLOW]

    while robot.is_turn_active():
        robot.screen.show_emoji(SILLY)
        robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, random.choice(colors))
        wait(110,MSEC)
        robot.screen.show_emoji(random.choice(face_list))
        wait(110,MSEC)

    robot.screen.show_emoji(SILLY)
    robot.led.off(ALL_LEDS)
    wait(.5,SECONDS)

    robot.move_for(25, 0, 50)

        robot.move_for(5, 180, 50)
        robot.screen.show_emoji(ANGRY)
        wait(50,MSEC)

    wait(.5,SECONDS)
    robot.led.off(ALL_LEDS)

    # act excited
    robot.screen.show_emoji(EXCITED)
    wait(.5,SECONDS)
    robot.sound.play(ACT_EXCITED)
    wait(115, MSEC)

    for angle in [3,-3,2,-2,2,-2,2,-2,1,-1]:
        if angle > 0:
            robot.screen.show_emoji(EXCITED, LOOK_RIGHT)
            robot.led.on([LED4,LED5,LED6], ORANGE)
            robot.led.off(LED1)
        else:

            robot.led.on([LED1,LED2,LED3], ORANGE)
            robot.led.off(LED6)
        robot.turn_for(RIGHT, angle)
        robot.led.off(ALL_LEDS)

    robot.screen.show_emoji(EXCITED, LOOK_FORWARD)
    wait(.5,SECONDS)

    random.choice(all_behaviours)()

reset_screen()
robot.screen.pressed(screen_pressed)

Project:      Advanced Controller
Description:  This project creates a more advanced controller setup that includes 
              adjustable speed and turn sensitivity for smoother and more precise control

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
"""

# Begin project code

turn_sensitivity = 0.5

def increase_turn_sensitivity():
    global turn_sensitivity
    turn_sensitivity += 0.1
    print("New turn sensitivity: " + str(turn_sensitivity))

def decrease_turn_sensitivity():
    global turn_sensitivity
    turn_sensitivity -= 0.1
    print("New turn sensitivity: " + str(turn_sensitivity))

def increase_speed_sensitivity():
    global speed_sensitivity
    speed_sensitivity += 0.1
    print("New speed sensitivity: " + str(speed_sensitivity))

def decrease_speed_sensitivity():
    global speed_sensitivity
    speed_sensitivity -= 0.1
    print("New speed sensitivity: " + str(speed_sensitivity))

def reset_sensitivities():
    global speed_sensitivity, turn_sensitivity
    speed_sensitivity = 0.5
    turn_sensitivity = 0.5
    print("Resetting to the default sensitivities")

# System event handlers
controller.button_left.pressed(increase_turn_sensitivity)
controller.button_right.pressed(decrease_turn_sensitivity)
controller.button_up.pressed(increase_speed_sensitivity)
controller.button_down.pressed(decrease_speed_sensitivity)
controller.button_stick.pressed(reset_sensitivities)

# Add 15ms delay to make sure events are registered correctly
wait(15, MSEC)

while True:
    # Move with controller input, using sensitivity values
    robot.move_with_vectors(
        controller.axis1.position() * speed_sensitivity,
        0,
        controller.axis2.position() * turn_sensitivity,
    )
    wait(5, MSEC)

"""
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Project:      AI Vision For Loops
Description:  This project uses a proper Python For Loop to iterate through AI Vision
              data to calculate a mid-point between an Orange Barrel and Blue Barrel

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
"""

# Begin project code
while True:
    # Create variables to store the centerX of each barrel, reset after each loop
    orange_center_x = None
    blue_center_x = None
    vision_data = robot.vision.get_data(ALL_CARGO)
    # Sets flags to track if barrels were detected in this loop
    found_orange_barrel = False
    found_blue_barrel = False
    # Use a for loop to iterate through the vision data
    for obj in vision_data:
        if obj.id == ORANGE_BARREL:
            # If an orange barrel was detected, assign the orange barrel centerX coordinate
            orange_center_x = obj.centerX
            found_orange_barrel = True
        if obj.id == BLUE_BARREL:
            # If a blue barrel was detected, assign the blue barrel centerX coordinate
            blue_center_x = obj.centerX
            found_blue_barrel = True
        wait(5, MSEC)
    # If the barrels are not detected, reset the centerX of the barrels
    if not found_orange_barrel:
        orange_center_x = None
    if not found_blue_barrel:
        blue_center_x = None
    # Check if we have centerX variables set for each barrel
    if orange_center_x is not None and blue_center_x is not None:
        # Calculate the midpoint coordinates
        mid_center_x = (orange_center_x + blue_center_x) / 2
        robot.screen.print_at("Midpoint centerX:", x=20, y=120)
        robot.screen.print_at(mid_center_x, x=80, y=150)
    elif orange_center_x is not None:
        robot.screen.print_at("Orange barrel", x=40, y=100)
        robot.screen.print_at("centerX: ", x=60, y=120)
        robot.screen.print_at(orange_center_x, x=80, y=150)
    elif blue_center_x is not None:
        robot.screen.print_at("Blue barrel", x=40, y=100)
        robot.screen.print_at("centerX: ", x=60, y=120)
        robot.screen.print_at(blue_center_x, x=80, y=150)
    else:
        robot.screen.print_at("No barrels found", x=20, y=120)
    wait(0.5, SECONDS)
    robot.screen.clear_screen(1, 1, BLUE)

              When a message is received from the linked AIM, it will be processed, and the corresponding 
              light will be turned on based on the receiving message.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
"""

# Begin project code
while True:
    # Check if the robot is connected to another AIM and if there is message in queue.
    if robot.link.is_connected() and robot.link.is_message_available():
        robot.led.off(ALL_LEDS)
        # Get latest message and check if it is a cargo object
        latest_message = robot.link.get_message()
        if latest_message == "SPORTS-BALL":
            robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, GREEN)
            robot.sound.play(COMPLETE)
        elif latest_message == "ORANGE-BARREL":
            robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, ORANGE)
            robot.sound.play(COMPLETE)
        elif latest_message == "BLUE-BARREL":
            robot.led.on(ALL_LEDS, BLUE)
            robot.sound.play(COMPLETE)
        else:
            # if the message is not one of the cargo objects, play fail sound.
            robot.sound.play(FAIL)
    wait(10, MSEC)
